subject: Geology
title: River Erosion
description: A quick win: understand River Erosion.

hook.text: A river starts as a trickle. Given millions of years, it can carve a canyon a mile deep. Water never stops cutting.

buildup.text: Rivers erode by abrasion, hydraulic action, and dissolution. They carry sediment that grinds the channel deeper.

discovery.text: Abrasion uses sand and pebbles as tools. The river hurls them against the bed and banks, grinding rock away.

twist.text: Floods do most of the erosion work. A single flood can reshape a river channel more than years of normal flow.

climax.text: Rivers are Earth's most powerful erosion agents. They move more sediment than wind, ice, and waves combined.

punchline.text: The gentlest force carves the deepest canyons.

quiz.question: How do rivers erode rock through abrasion?
options: By hurling sand and pebbles against the channel bed
